title: Chunk curriculum into smaller pieces/chapters with a dependency tree & table of contents in a separate md file so we can parse it quicker
description:
The induction_curriculum.md file is currently 3897 lines and difficult to parse/navigate. Refactor to improve organization:

OBJECTIVES:
- Split curriculum into modular chapter files (one file per lesson or per phase)
- Create master table of contents with dependency tree
- Improve parsability and navigation
- Maintain all existing content

PROPOSED STRUCTURE:
- curriculum/README.md (TOC + dependency visualization)
- curriculum/phase1/ (lessons 01-10)
- curriculum/phase2/ (lessons 11-18)
- curriculum/phase3/ (lessons 19-26)
- curriculum/phase4/ (lessons 27-32)
- curriculum/phase5/ (lessons 33-38)
- curriculum/phase6/ (lessons 39-44)

BENEFITS:
- Faster file parsing (smaller files)
- Better git diffs (changes isolated to specific lessons)
- Easier navigation with TOC
- Clear dependency visualization
- Modular updates without conflicts

REQUIREMENTS:
- Preserve all 68 photo placeholders
- Maintain cross-references between lessons
- Generate dependency graph (e.g., Lesson 28 depends on Lesson 27)
- Include metadata (duration, prerequisites, tools needed)
- Keep original file as reference until migration complete

title: Verify selected components in the induction_curriculum.md can actually work together for the intended project (which is documented in the md file). The datasheet pdfs are in the md file. Use reasonable strategies to prove these components make sense to go together.
Refine project plan
description: This project is aimed at creating a clone of a breville control freak. It's being built by a relative novice so the build is being broken down into a course. The course starts from zero and builds up to a functional clone. The project prioritizes modularity, iteration, verification, simulation, and safety.
design:
## Architecture

The project is a **simulation-first, modular induction cooking system** designed as a 44-lesson educational curriculum. It guides developers from basic component characterization through full system integration, emphasizing safety, verification, and professional engineering practices.

**System Overview:**
- **Target:** 1500-2000W high-precision induction cooker (Breville Control Freak clone)
- **Methodology:** Simulation-first (validate in SPICE before hardware)
- **Controller:** ESP32-S3 dual-core MCU
- **Power Stage:** Half-bridge resonant inverter (30-40kHz)
- **Safety:** Hardware interlocks + firmware redundancy + galvanic isolation

**Six-Phase Structure:**
1. **Phase I (Lessons 1-10):** Simulation environment & component characterization
2. **Phase II (Lessons 11-18):** Power stage simulation & resonant tank design
3. **Phase III (Lessons 19-26):** Control system & sensing simulation
4. **Phase IV (Lessons 27-32):** Firmware development & algorithm validation
5. **Phase V (Lessons 33-38):** PCB design & manufacturing preparation
6. **Phase VI (Lessons 39-44):** Hardware assembly, integration & calibration

## Components

### Power Electronics
- **IKW40N120H3:** 1200V 40A IGBT (2× for half-bridge)
- **UCC21550:** Isolated gate driver with hardware dead-time
- **Resonant Tank:** 50µH coil + 470nF capacitor bank (33kHz resonance)

### Auxiliary Power
- **LMR51430:** 24V→5V wide-Vin buck converter (1.1MHz switching)
- **LDO + Pi-filter:** 5V→3.3V low-noise supply for MCU
- **UCC14140-Q1:** 12V→22V/4V isolated dual-output for high-side driver

### Sensing & Control
- **ESP32-S3:** Dual-core MCU with MCPWM peripheral
- **MAX31865:** RTD-to-digital converter (15-bit resolution)
- **ADuM1250:** I2C isolator for probe safety
- **AMC1200:** Isolated current sensing amplifier
- **CT:** Current transformer for tank current monitoring

### Safety & UI
- **Hardware Interlocks:** LM393 comparator for over-temperature/over-current
- **Fan Control:** PWM-controlled active cooling with tachometer feedback
- **User Interface:** Rotary encoder + OLED/LCD display
- **RTD Probe:** Galvanically isolated temperature sensor

### Mechanical/Thermal
- **Heatsinks:** Thermal design for 2kW dissipation
- **Active Cooling:** Temperature-controlled fan
- **Thermal Interface:** Thermal vias, PCB copper pours

## Dependencies

**Software/Tools:**
- KiCad 8.0 (schematic capture, PCB layout, integrated SPICE simulation)
- ngspice (circuit simulation engine)
- ESP-IDF (ESP32 firmware development framework)
- Python 3.x (behavioral modeling, PID tuning scripts)
- Manufacturer SPICE models (IKW40N120H3, UCC21550, LMR51430, UCC14140-Q1)

**Design Constraints:**
- AC Input: 120Vrms 60Hz (US mains)
- DC Bus Voltage: 310V (rectified, with PFC assumed)
- Operating Frequency: 30-40kHz (adjustable for ZVS tuning)
- Target Temperature Control: ±0.5°C steady-state error, zero overshoot
- Isolation Requirements: >10MΩ @ 500V (user-accessible probe)
- Safety Standards: UL/IEC compliance (assumed, not explicitly documented)

**Knowledge Prerequisites:**
- Basic circuit theory (Ohm's law, AC analysis, impedance)
- SPICE simulation fundamentals
- Resonant converter theory (LC tanks, ZVS)
- Microcontroller programming (C/C++)
- PCB design principles (isolation, thermal management)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 68 photo placeholders replaced with actual screenshots/diagrams
- [ ] Lesson 29 (PLL) implementation completed with detailed algorithm
- [ ] Lesson 31 (State Machine) completed with full state transition diagram
- [ ] EMI/EMC filtering strategy lesson added (dedicated section)
- [ ] Soft-start circuit (Lesson 09) expanded with relay bypass and thermal modeling
- [ ] Manufacturing tolerance analysis section added (worst-case validation)
- [ ] Component derating calculations documented
- [ ] Firmware code examples completed for all critical algorithms
- [ ] Test data templates populated with example measurements
- [ ] Component selection rationale documented (justify specific part numbers)
- [ ] Debugging/troubleshooting guide added
- [ ] BOM cost analysis section added

title: Complete Lesson 29: PLL implementation for ZVS tracking
description:
Lesson 29 currently has only section headers. Need to add:
- PLL theory and phase detection methods
- Digital PLL implementation on ESP32-S3
- Frequency tracking algorithm for maintaining ZVS
- SPICE/Python simulation of PLL behavior
- Code examples for ESP32 timer integration
- Validation criteria and test procedures

title: Complete Lesson 31: State machine diagram and implementation
description:
Lesson 31 lists states and transitions but lacks visual diagram. Need to:
- Create comprehensive state transition diagram
- Document error recovery sequences
- Add detailed state entry/exit conditions
- Include timing diagrams for critical transitions
- Provide complete C implementation example
- Add test cases for state machine validation

title: Add EMI/EMC filtering and compliance section
description:
Missing dedicated EMI/EMC lesson. Should include:
- Conducted emissions filtering (CM/DM chokes)
- Radiated emissions mitigation strategies
- PCB layout rules for high-frequency noise
- Common-mode vs. differential-mode analysis
- SPICE simulation of filter effectiveness
- Regulatory compliance checklist (UL/FCC/CE)
- Measurement procedures and acceptance criteria

title: Expand Lesson 09: Soft-start circuit with relay bypass
description:
Lesson 09 is minimal, only covers basic NTC model. Needs:
- Detailed NTC thermistor thermal time-constant analysis
- Relay bypass circuit design and timing
- PTC vs NTC comparison and selection criteria
- Inrush current calculations
- SPICE simulation with thermal coupling
- Relay contact protection (snubber/MOV)
- Failure mode analysis (stuck relay, open NTC)

title: Add manufacturing tolerance and worst-case analysis
description:
Missing critical manufacturing quality analysis. Need to add:
- Component tolerance stack-up analysis
- Worst-case temperature sweep validation (-40°C to +85°C)
- Component derating calculations (IGBT, capacitors, magnetics)
- Sensitivity analysis for critical parameters (resonant frequency, dead-time)
- Production yield modeling
- Statistical process control metrics
- Qualification test procedures (thermal cycling, vibration, humidity)

title: Generate and integrate 68 photo placeholders
description:
Curriculum has 68 [PHOTO PLACEHOLDER] markers needing real screenshots. Categories:
- KiCad schematic screenshots (symbol chooser, properties, hierarchical sheets)
- Simulation waveforms (oscilloscope plots, bode plots)
- SPICE simulation results (transient analysis, AC sweep)
- 3D PCB renders (layout, stackup visualization)
- Physical hardware photos (breadboard tests, final assembly)
- Measurement instrumentation (multimeter, oscilloscope captures)

This task requires:
1. Building actual KiCad schematics for each lesson
2. Running simulations and capturing results
3. Taking high-quality screenshots with annotations
4. Replacing placeholder text with figure references

title: Document component selection rationale and BOM
description:
Add section explaining why specific components were chosen:
- Cost vs. performance tradeoffs
- Alternative components considered
- Availability and supply chain considerations
- Second-source options for critical components
- Complete BOM with:
  * Manufacturer part numbers
  * Quantity required
  * Unit cost and total project cost
  * Supplier/distributor information
  * Lead time considerations
  * Recommended substitutes

title: Add debugging and troubleshooting guide
description:
Create comprehensive troubleshooting section covering:
- Common failure modes and symptoms
- Debug procedures for each subsystem
- Test point locations and expected values
- Oscilloscope probe placement guidelines
- Error code interpretation
- Safety precautions during debug
- Tools and equipment required
- Step-by-step debug workflows for:
  * No power
  * No heating
  * Erratic temperature control
  * Hardware faults triggering
  * Communication failures

title: Complete firmware code examples for all algorithms
description:
Expand firmware sections with complete, production-ready C implementations:
- Pan detection algorithm (Lesson 27)
- PID controller with anti-windup (Lesson 28)
- PLL for frequency tracking (Lesson 29)
- Watchdog timer integration (Lesson 30)
- State machine (Lesson 31)
- Integration and testing framework (Lesson 32)

Each should include:
- Well-commented source code
- Unit test examples
- Performance benchmarks (CPU usage, timing)
- Memory usage analysis
- Configuration parameters and tuning guidelines

title: Verify components and create modular ngspice models
description: Each simulation should be performed in ngspice first and turned into a black box module according to its datasheet and verified as much as possible. It should be as modular as possible but also use the kicad_sym and lib files that exist. It is possible they contain errors so verify their correctness first.
